goal: Launch it with Department of war

campaign_brief.objective: Secure LSAD (Low-Signature Autonomous Detection) as an approved, fielded capability within Department of War programs of record by establishing technical credibility, completing a successful pilot/OT prototype, and driving a transition-to-production decision within 18 months.
campaign_brief.target_audience: DoW program managers, PEO/PM acquisition leads, combatant command capability developers, DIU/AFWERX/Army xTech transition officers, and warfighter end-users in ISR and force-protection roles.
campaign_brief.key_message: LSAD delivers passive, low-signature autonomous threat detection that operates in GPS-denied, EW-contested environments without revealing the operator's position.
campaign_brief.proof_point: Demonstrated 94% detection accuracy with sub-2-second classification latency across 600+ field test hours in contested-spectrum exercises, validated by an independent government test range.
campaign_brief.call_to_action: Schedule a classified capability demonstration and OTA prototype scoping session with our defense solutions team.

primary_persona.role: DoW Program Manager / PEO Acquisition Lead (O-5 to O-6 / GS-14 to GS-15)
pain_points: Existing sensors emit detectable signatures that compromise warfighter survivability in contested environments
Legacy ISR systems fail or degrade in GPS-denied and EW-jammed conditions
Pressure to deliver fielded capability within constrained budget cycles and aggressive transition timelines
Risk-averse acquisition culture penalizes adopting unproven vendors lacking TRL maturity and ATO pathway
Integration burden with existing C2 architectures and data standards (e.g., MOSA, OMS/UCI)
information_sources: Service-specific industry days and PEO solicitations on SAM.gov
DIU, AFWERX, Army xTech, and SOCOM SOFWERX engagement channels
Defense trade press (Breaking Defense, Defense News, C4ISRNET)
AUSA, SOF Week, Sea-Air-Space, and AFA Warfare Symposium conferences
Peer program managers and JCIDS capability working groups

buying_committee.their_question: Can this transition to a program of record on my timeline and budget?
buying_committee.content_needed: Transition roadmap with TRL/MRL assessment, OTA-to-production pathway, and cost-per-unit at scale

buying_committee.their_question: What contract vehicle and authority lets us award this quickly and compliantly?
buying_committee.content_needed: OTA/SBIR Phase III eligibility brief, GSA/sole-source justification template, and CMMC/DFARS compliance attestation

buying_committee.their_question: Does it meet MOSA standards and integrate with our existing architecture?
buying_committee.content_needed: Interface control document, OMS/UCI conformance brief, and cybersecurity/ATO RMF artifacts

buying_committee.their_question: Will this actually work in the field and reduce my operational risk?
buying_committee.content_needed: Field test after-action reports, operator-narrated demo video, and CONOPS use-case scenarios

buying_committee.their_question: What is the cyber risk and ATO posture of this system?
buying_committee.content_needed: RMF security control package, supply-chain risk assessment, and SBOM documentation

icp.company_size: Government programs with $5M+ annual capability budgets within PEOs and combatant commands
geographies: United States (CONUS test ranges and PEO hubs)

messaging_hierarchy.headline: Detect Threats Without Being Detected
messaging_hierarchy.subheadline: LSAD: passive autonomous sensing for GPS-denied, EW-contested battlespaces.
messaging_hierarchy.body_message: LSAD (Low-Signature Autonomous Detection) gives warfighters persistent threat awareness while emitting near-zero detectable signature. Engineered for contested and degraded environments, LSAD autonomously classifies threats in under two seconds without transmitting the operator's position, integrates with MOSA-compliant C2 systems, and arrives with a clear OTA-to-production transition path and RMF/ATO documentation ready for accreditation.
messaging_hierarchy.tone: Authoritative, technically precise, mission-focused, and credible without hype

channels.channel: Direct PEO/Program Office Engagement & Industry Days
channels.rationale: Defense acquisition is relationship- and requirements-driven; direct engagement with program offices is the highest-yield path to capturing requirements and securing OTA opportunities.
channels.kpi: Number of qualified program-office meetings converting to OTA scoping discussions

channels.rationale: AUSA, SOF Week, and Sea-Air-Space are where capability developers and warfighters evaluate technology; classified demos build technical trust unavailable through other channels.
channels.kpi: Number of completed live/classified demonstrations to target programs

channels.rationale: Breaking Defense, C4ISRNET, and Defense News shape narrative and credibility among program managers who research vendors before engagement.
channels.kpi: Earned-media placements and content engagement from .mil/.gov domains

channels.rationale: Precisely targets named acquisition and technical roles within DoW programs to reinforce messaging between in-person touchpoints.
channels.kpi: Engagement rate and meeting-request conversions from named target accounts

channel_recommendation.channel_sequence: Lead with trade media and thought leadership to establish credibility, run ABM digital in parallel to warm named accounts, then concentrate spend on direct program-office engagement and culminate in classified demonstrations at flagship conferences timed to budget cycles.

allocation.channel: Direct PEO/Program Office Engagement & Industry Days

content_creative_plan.hero_asset: An operator-narrated, government-test-range field demonstration video showing LSAD detecting and classifying threats in a GPS-denied, EW-contested scenario with overlaid performance metrics (94% accuracy, sub-2s latency).

supporting_assets.purpose: Leave-behind for program managers summarizing performance, transition path, and compliance posture
supporting_assets.specs: Single-page PDF, FOUO-compatible, MOSA/RMF callouts, classification banner-ready

supporting_assets.purpose: Establish TRL maturity and architecture for chief engineers and technical authorities
supporting_assets.specs: 12-18 page PDF with ICD summary, OMS/UCI conformance, test methodology

supporting_assets.purpose: Show contracting officers and PMs the OTA/SBIR Phase III pathway
supporting_assets.specs: 8-slide deck, vehicle options, cost-at-scale model, CMMC attestation

supporting_assets.purpose: Provide independent validation evidence to the full buying committee
supporting_assets.specs: Government-range AAR summary, releasable metrics, methodology appendix

content_calendar.action: Publish technical white paper and seed to defense trade press under embargo
Launch hero demonstration video and amplify via ABM LinkedIn to named accounts
Release thought-leadership byline in C4ISRNET on low-signature ISR in contested environments
Distribute capability one-pager and transition brief at industry day engagements
Publish first CONOPS scenario animation; begin demo scheduling outreach
Release field test after-action report and host webinar for capability developers
Deliver classified demonstrations at flagship conference; capture warfighter testimonials
Publish case study from pilot engagement and launch transition-decision nurture sequence

actions: Complete independent government-range validation testing and finalize AAR
Secure CMMC Level 2 certification and assemble RMF/ATO artifact package
Map target PEOs and align LSAD to active and emerging capability requirements
Identify and confirm OTA consortium memberships and SBIR Phase III eligibility
Produce hero video, white paper, one-pager, and transition brief
Execute coordinated trade-media embargo lift and thought-leadership publication
Activate ABM digital campaign against named acquisition and technical roles
Host classified capability demonstration at flagship conference (e.g., AUSA or SOF Week)
Open direct program-office engagements and submit white papers to active solicitations
Capture leads and qualify into OTA scoping pipeline
Convert qualified programs into OTA prototype agreements and pilot deployments
Run operator-in-the-loop pilots and collect field performance data and testimonials
Publish pilot case study and nurture full buying committee toward transition decision
Pursue transition-to-production planning and budget-cycle alignment for follow-on funding
Expand to adjacent PEOs and allied partner nations via Five Eyes channels

launch_timeline.defense_timing_note: US Government fiscal year runs Oct 1 - Sep 30. Align major demonstrations and OTA proposals to Q3 FY (Apr-Jun) when programs identify end-of-year funds and to Q1 FY (Oct-Dec) when new appropriations land. Avoid relying on Q4 FY (Jul-Sep) closeout for new starts, and account for continuing-resolution risk that can freeze new-start funding early in the fiscal year; have the prototype/OTA pathway ready so unobligated end-of-year funds can be captured quickly.

sales_enablement.one_pager_summary: LSAD (Low-Signature Autonomous Detection) provides warfighters persistent, passive threat detection in GPS-denied and EW-contested environments without emitting a detectable signature. Field-validated at 94% accuracy and sub-2-second classification latency, MOSA-compliant for C2 integration, and delivered with a clear OTA-to-production transition path and RMF/ATO-ready documentation.
talk_track: Open with the survivability problem: 'In contested environments, every emission your current sensors make is a beacon to the adversary—LSAD detects without revealing your position.'
Establish proof: cite independent government-range validation—94% accuracy, sub-2s latency across 600+ field hours in EW-contested conditions.
Reduce adoption risk: walk through the OTA/SBIR Phase III pathway, CMMC compliance, and RMF/ATO artifact package that de-risks accreditation.
Demonstrate fit: connect LSAD's MOSA/OMS/UCI conformance to the program's existing architecture and integration requirements.
Close to next step: propose a classified capability demonstration and a 90-day OTA prototype scoping session aligned to the program's budget cycle.

objection_handling.objection: You're an unproven vendor—we can't risk our program on immature technology.
objection_handling.response: LSAD has completed 600+ hours of independent government-range testing at TRL 7 with a documented AAR. We propose a low-risk OTA prototype with defined exit criteria so you validate performance before any production commitment.

objection_handling.objection: We don't have a contract vehicle or funding to move quickly.
objection_handling.response: We hold OTA consortium memberships and qualify for SBIR Phase III sole-source authority, which lets you award without full-and-open competition. We'll also align the proposal to your end-of-fiscal-year unobligated funds to accelerate award.

objection_handling.objection: How does this integrate with our existing C2 and pass cybersecurity accreditation?
objection_handling.response: LSAD is MOSA-compliant with documented OMS/UCI interfaces and ships with a complete RMF security control package, SBOM, and supply-chain risk assessment to streamline your ATO and authorizing official review.

objection_handling.objection: Other sensors already cover this mission—why do we need LSAD?
objection_handling.response: Existing active sensors emit signatures that compromise survivability and degrade in EW-jammed, GPS-denied conditions. LSAD operates passively and autonomously in exactly those environments where current systems fail—it complements, not duplicates, your sensing layer.

demo_sequence: Set the scenario: brief a GPS-denied, EW-contested operational vignette tied to the program's mission
Show passive detection: demonstrate LSAD identifying and classifying threats with zero detectable emission
Display performance metrics live: accuracy, classification latency, and false-alarm rate on screen
Demonstrate C2 integration: pipe LSAD output into a MOSA-compliant common operating picture
Walk the transition path: review OTA pathway, ATO artifacts, and cost-at-scale, then schedule prototype scoping

kpis.primary_kpi: Number of LSAD OTA prototype agreements signed with DoW programs within 12 months (target: 3)

channel_kpis.channel: Direct PEO/Program Office Engagement & Industry Days
channel_kpis.kpi: Qualified program-office meetings converting to OTA scoping discussions
Completed live/classified demonstrations to target programs
Earned-media placements and engagement from .mil/.gov domains
Meeting-request conversions from named target accounts

kpis.reporting_cadence: Weekly pipeline stand-up during launch period; monthly executive dashboard; quarterly business review aligned to fiscal-year budget milestones

risks.risk: Continuing resolution freezes new-start funding, stalling OTA awards
risks.mitigation: Prioritize programs with existing OTA vehicles and unobligated prior-year funds; position LSAD as a prototype add-on to funded programs rather than a new start; maintain a ready proposal package to capture end-of-year sweep-up funds.

risks.risk: Failure to secure ATO/RMF accreditation delays fielding
risks.mitigation: Engage an authorizing official early, complete the RMF control package and SBOM pre-launch, and pursue an interim ATO or reciprocity from an existing accredited environment to accelerate the pathway.

risks.risk: Independent test performance falls short of advertised metrics under new conditions
risks.mitigation: Scope OTA prototypes with realistic, condition-specific exit criteria; maintain conservative public claims tied only to validated test data; reserve engineering capacity to tune for program-specific environments.

risks.risk: Incumbent vendor lock-in or competing program of record displaces LSAD
risks.mitigation: Position LSAD as a complementary passive layer rather than a replacement, emphasize MOSA interoperability, and target capability gaps the incumbent cannot address in contested/GPS-denied conditions.

pivot_triggers: Fewer than 4 qualified program-office scoping discussions by end of month 6 — reallocate budget from media to direct engagement and DIU/SOFWERX channels
Continuing resolution extends past Q2 FY — shift focus to allied Five Eyes partners and FMS-eligible opportunities
Demo-to-OTA conversion below 25% — revisit messaging and add deeper technical proof assets for chief-engineer audiences
ATO timeline exceeds 6 months on lead program — pivot lead pursuit to a program with established accredited environment
